etymology.description: 止("foot") + 凵("cave") = to step out of a cave -> exit
etymology.interpretation: The character '出' is composed of '止'("foot") and '凵'("cave"), symbolizing the action of stepping out of a cave, which aligns well with the meaning 'to exit'. This ancient imagery effectively conveys the concept of going out or emerging.

examples.chinese: 我们计划下周末出门(我們計劃下週末出門)。
examples.english: We plan to go out next weekend.

examples.chinese: 他每天早上七点出门上班(他每天早上七點出門上班)。
examples.pinyin: Tā měitiān zǎoshang qī diǎn chūmén shàngbān.
examples.english: He goes out to work at seven every morning.

examples.chinese: 请等我几分钟，我马上就出来(請等我幾分鐘，我馬上就出來)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng děng wǒ jǐ fēnzhōng, wǒ mǎshàng jiù chūlái.
examples.english: Please wait for me a few minutes, I will come out right away.
